data.body:
## Description

**What**: Refactor ticket-graph.py so that link and deps operations load all non-archived tickets once and share the pre-loaded state dict across _find_direct_blockers, child lookup, and transitive blocker detection.
**Why**: ticket-graph.py currently performs 2-3 independent full-tracker scans per invocation — each calling os.listdir + reduce_ticket on every ticket directory. Consolidating into one shared batch reduction eliminates redundant I/O.
**Scope**:
- IN: Consolidating _find_direct_blockers, child lookup, _get_all_blocked_by into a single reduce_all_tickets call; restructuring BFS to use pre-loaded state dict
- OUT: Changes to ticket-transition.sh (Story 2); changes to ticket-link.sh's own per-ticket scans (separate concern)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, ticket-graph.py link and deps operations perform at most one reduce_all_tickets(exclude_archived=True) per invocation (down from 2-3 independent scans)
  ← Satisfies: "ticket-graph.py link and deps operations perform at most one batch reduction of non-archived tickets per invocation"
- When this story is complete, _find_direct_blockers, child lookup, and transitive blocker detection share a single pre-loaded ticket state dict
  ← Satisfies: "ticket-graph.py link and deps operations perform at most one batch reduction of non-archived tickets per invocation"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Scope] ticket-link.sh is the CLI entry point for ticket link and performs its own per-ticket reducer calls via _is_duplicate_link and ticket_read_status. These are separate from ticket-graph.py and are not covered by this story's consolidation. If ticket link performance is also a concern, ticket-link.sh's scan pattern needs separate attention.
- [Performance] _get_all_blocked_by (ticket-graph.py line 350-411) performs a full os.listdir + _reduce_ticket scan of ALL ticket directories on EACH iteration of its BFS loop. Consolidating to one reduce_all_tickets call requires restructuring the BFS to use a pre-loaded state dict rather than scanning per hop. This is more invasive than consolidating _find_direct_blockers.
- [Reliability] Transitive blocker traversal must NOT skip archived tickets in the middle of dependency chains — only skip as scan entry points
- [Maintainability] Consolidated batch scan must remain readable

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
